game_name: Dragon Quest VI: Realms of Revelation

design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather party members → explore dual worlds (Real/Dream) → defeat boss monsters → advance story through character leveling and class mastery
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Amnesia-driven narrative mystery: heroes must recover identity while uncovering the link between Real World and Dream World threats
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Traditional hero's journey with party building; mastery progression through multi-tiered class system allowing player-driven character customization
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Dual-world structure creates parallel map exploration; class system requires intentional battle-grinding to unlock abilities; emphasis on party composition and class synergy over pure stat progression

difficulty.checkpointing: Turn-based battles allow tactical pausing; no explicit checkpoint system documented
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through class selection, party composition, and intentional level-grinding before boss encounters; traditional RPG structure allows players to over-prepare

progression.upgrade_system: Multi-tiered class system with 8 levels per class. Starter classes unlock hybrid classes; hybrid classes unlock Hero class. Class mastery grants permanent stat increases and spell/skill retention across class changes
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story flags (boss defeats) unlock new areas; Alltrades Abbey gates class system access; hybrid classes unlock via mastering specific starter class combinations; two secret classes unlocked via consumable books
replayability_hooks: Mini-medals hidden throughout worlds for item trading
Slime Arena and Best Dresser Contest mini-games
Day/night system triggers specific story events
Wagon system allows flexible party swapping

world_building_techniques: Dual-world structure (Real World vs Dream World) creates parallel geography and thematic contrast
Amnesia mechanic weaves character identity discovery into main narrative
NPC dialogue reveals world lore and character motivations
Day/night cycle ties specific story events to time mechanics
Medieval fantasy setting with castles and knights establishes genre expectations

boss_design.philosophy: Story-gated encounters; defeat required to progress. Bosses serve as narrative milestones tied to dual-world revelation arc

faction_indicators: Party member recruitment creates loyalty/companionship bonds
Class choices signal player identity (Warrior vs Sage vs Merchant specialization)
danger_signals: Dual-world threat escalation (Real World + Dream World convergence peril)
Amnesia mechanic creates vulnerability—heroes must recover knowledge to face danger
environmental_storytelling: Parallel Real/Dream World maps show how worlds mirror yet diverge
NPCs' presence/absence between worlds reflects dream vs reality themes
Medieval architecture (castles, towns) grounds fantasy narrative
Hidden mini-medals encourage world exploration and discovery
